description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface. The grooves are deep and create a ribbed texture, likely intended to provide traction or drainage. The block is solid and has a dark, speckled stone appearance, suggesting it is made from a dense material such as granite or basalt. The sides are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall form is geometric and precise, indicating it was manufactured or cut with care. This type of block is commonly used in urban environments for functional and aesthetic purposes, such as in pavements, plazas, or as part of a barrier system.
description: A dark, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, giving it a ribbed and textured appearance. The sides are smooth and the block appears dense and heavy.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface and the solid, smooth front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the smooth side face visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the grooved top and solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the parallel grooves and the smooth side face.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a tactile or drainage barrier
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: aligned flush with adjacent pavement or stone blocks
